A partner by the side
of every SME

about.title:
Your partner &
companion
for SMEs in Thailand.
about.description: Fifteen years on the ground in Bangkok. We support Japanese businesses across languages, industries, and growth stages.

services.title:
Four pillars
for every stage in Thailand.

items.title: Company Setup
items.description: BOI, local entities, and representative offices — we design the entry scheme that fits your business model.

items.title: Accounting & Tax
items.description: From monthly bookkeeping to tax filings and audits, aligned with both Thai standards and your Japan HQ.

items.title: Accounting System Implementation
items.description: Cloud accounting / ERP selection, chart-of-accounts design, and month-end automation that works for both local ops and Japan HQ.

items.title: Management Consulting
items.description: Management accounting, KPIs, and performance turnaround — we get into the operating reality with you.

reasons.title: Fully trilingual
reasons.description: Japanese, English, and Thai delivered by one team — HQ, local staff, and authorities stay in sync.

reasons.title: Focused on Japanese SMEs
reasons.description: We design operations specifically for Japan-affiliated SMEs in Thailand, sharing pitfalls and shortcuts from day one.

reasons.title: One-stop
reasons.description: Accounting, setup, and consulting under one roof — one contact, zero handover loss.

reasons.title: Fifteen years on the ground
reasons.description: Our local network across authorities, banks, and specialists opens the fastest viable route.

cases.title: Selected work.
cases.description: Our strength is supporting multi-location, storefront businesses — restaurants, salons, clinics, agencies — where daily operations and the numbers are tightly linked, with hands-on accounting, tax and HR support on the ground in Thailand.

items.title: Reconciling cash and delivery sales across 3 outlets for per-store P/L
items.summary: Cash and delivery-platform deposits could no longer be reconciled, leaving per-store profitability invisible. POS integration and a faster monthly close gave head office the numbers to manage by.
items.challenge: As the group grew to three Japanese restaurants, cash sales, deposits from delivery platforms such as Grab and LINE MAN, and in-store card payments were each tracked separately, and the month-end reconciliation had broken down. With commission-net deposits impossible to match against sales, neither per-store margins nor even total company revenue could be stated with confidence.
approach: Set reconciliation rules across POS, daily sales and deposits
Allocated delivery commissions and promo costs by outlet
Cloud accounting to speed the close and output per-store P/L

results.label: Time to monthly close (about 15 days faster)

items.title: Unifying commission pay with social security and withholding tax
items.summary: Stylist commission calculations depended on one person, and social security and personal income tax withholding contained errors. Outsourced payroll and HR cleanup made the monthly cycle accurate and on time.
items.challenge: At two salons serving Japanese expatriates, the store manager calculated commission pay tied to nomination and service revenue by hand, so each month brought variance and delays. Social Security Office (SSO) deductions and personal income tax withholding (PND1) also contained errors that demanded constant correction.
approach: Defined commission logic and standardized the calculation template
Outsourced monthly SSO and PND1 withholding and filing
Multilingual payslips and a clear disbursement flow

results.label: Errors in withholding and social security (post-fix)
Monthly payroll workload removed from stores

items.title: Untangling revenue recognition and drug inventory for a clinic
items.summary: Insurance and self-pay treatment had different cash cycles, blurring revenue recognition, and drug inventory was untracked. A new accounting framework made department-level P/L and stock visible.
items.challenge: At this Japanese-affiliated clinic, cashless billing to insurers was mixed with cash and card payments from self-pay treatment, and revenue was recognized at inconsistent times. Pharmaceuticals and medical supplies were not tracked in the books either, leaving cost of goods and wastage unknown.
approach: Separated revenue recognition for insurance and self-pay
Introduced department- and segment-level P/L management
Built stock-count and cost-recognition flow for drugs

results.label: Insurance receivable settlement cycle, now tracked
Frequency of drug stock-count and costing

items.title: Project-level cost control to surface loss-making jobs
items.summary: Costs were not tracked per project, so unprofitable jobs only surfaced at year-end. Project P/L, WHT management and tighter receivables let the team judge profitability before taking work on.
items.challenge: At this Japanese-affiliated agency, production, media and subcontracting costs were booked across projects, so gross margin per job was invisible. Withholding tax (WHT 3%) on subcontractors was often missed, and combined with slow collections, cash flow was chronically tight.
approach: Tagged and aggregated costs by project code
Set up 3% subcontractor WHT and PND3/53 remittance
Managed the billing-to-collection cycle to improve cash flow

results.label: When profitability can now be judged

items.title: Moving from Excel and paper invoices to PEAK cloud accounting
items.summary: Invoicing and bookkeeping ran on Excel and paper, so the numbers lagged until month-end. Rolling out Thailand's PEAK cloud accounting delivered real-time visibility and far less bookkeeping work.
items.challenge: Sales and purchase invoices were created in Excel and filed on paper, and bookkeeping relied on manual entry, so the monthly figures were not firm until late the following month. VAT and withholding tax were tallied by hand each time, carrying the risk of entry errors and double-counting.
approach: Rolled out PEAK to digitize invoicing and bookkeeping
Configured automatic VAT/WHT tallying and reporting
Tailored the chart of accounts and workflow to the company

results.label: Days to finalize the monthly trial balance

items.title: Guiding an F&B market entry through to license approval
items.summary: We supported everything from DBD registration and VAT registration to the food business license and the expatriate's visa and work permit. Running the administrative steps in parallel enabled a fast launch.
items.challenge: Entering Thailand's F&B sector for the first time, the Japanese parent had no clear picture of company structure, foreign-ownership rules or the licenses required, and could not judge which steps to take in what order. Progress had to be managed backward from the planned opening date.
approach: Handled DBD company registration and tax/VAT registration
Supported food license and signboard tax applications
Obtained the expatriate's visa and work permit

results.label: Single point for setup, tax, license and visa

items.title: Closing the company in an orderly liquidation on exit
items.summary: We carried out employee terminations, tax clearance and liquidation registration step by step in line with the law. The company was wound up without dispute, completing the parent's exit on plan.
items.challenge: A parent-company restructuring meant withdrawing from Thailand, but the required steps and timeline were unclear — statutory severance under labor law, settlement of unpaid taxes and VAT, appointment of a liquidator and creditor notices among them.
approach: Carried out lawful severance and separation procedures
Cancelled VAT registration and obtained tax clearance
Completed liquidator appointment, creditor notice and registration

process.title:
From first call
to continuous support.

steps.title: Free consultation
steps.description: A 30–45 min online call. Share your current state and goals.

steps.title: Deep-dive interview
steps.description: We map your numbers, organization, and workflows in detail.

steps.title: Proposal & quote
steps.description: Hypothesis, scope, and pricing — clearly on the table.

steps.title: Contract & kickoff
steps.description: Onboarding completes and operations start within ~2 weeks.

steps.title: Ongoing support
steps.description: Monthly reviews, with quarterly strategy updates layered on top.

cta.title:
Start with a
free consultation.
cta.description: From sharpening the problem to choosing the right entry scheme — 30 minutes is enough to move forward.

hero.title:
Privacy
Policy
hero.description: MIRAI BizLab Co., Ltd. is committed to handling and protecting your personal data responsibly.

intro: MIRAI BizLab Co., Ltd. ("we", "us", "our") handles the personal data we collect through this website (www.miraibizlab.co.th) and in the course of providing our services in accordance with Thailand's Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562 ("PDPA") and other applicable laws.
This policy explains what personal data we collect, how we use it, when we share it, and the rights available to you.

controller.heading: Data Controller
lines: 954/1724 The Metropolis Building, 3rd Floor, Room No. M3, Moo. 9, Thepharak, Muang Samutprakarn, Samutprakarn 10270, Thailand

sections.heading: Personal data we collect
paragraphs: We may collect the following personal data:
bullets: Information you provide through our contact form (name, company name, email address, phone number, and the content of your enquiry)
The content of conversations you enter into our AI chat support
Information collected automatically when you use the website (IP address, browser and device type, pages viewed, access time, and identifiers such as cookies)

sections.heading: Purposes of use
paragraphs: We use the personal data we collect for the following purposes:
bullets: Responding to enquiries and consultations, preparing quotations, and providing our services
Maintaining and improving service quality, and improving and analysing the website
Sending you notices and relevant information where you have consented
Complying with the law, preventing misuse, and protecting our legitimate rights and interests

sections.heading: Legal basis for processing
paragraphs: Under the PDPA, we process personal data on one of the following legal bases: performance of a contract, the legitimate interests of us or a third party, compliance with a legal obligation, or your consent. Where we rely on consent, you may withdraw it at any time.

sections.heading: Disclosure and processors
paragraphs: We do not sell or rent your personal data to third parties without your consent. However, we may entrust the handling of personal data to the following trusted service providers (processors) to the extent necessary to provide our services:
bullets: Where disclosure is required by law, or necessary to protect rights and safety

sections.heading: Cross-border data transfers
paragraphs: Some of the processors listed above are located outside Thailand (e.g. in the United States). Where personal data is transferred internationally, we handle it under the appropriate safeguards required by the PDPA.

sections.heading: Cookies and analytics
paragraphs: Our website may use cookies and similar technologies (including analytics) to understand usage and improve our services. You can refuse cookies through your browser settings, but some features may not function correctly as a result.

sections.heading: Data retention
paragraphs: We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to achieve the purposes of use or as required by law, and we securely delete or anonymise it when retention is no longer necessary.

sections.heading: Security measures
paragraphs: We implement reasonable and appropriate technical and organisational security measures to prevent unauthorised access, loss, destruction, alteration, and leakage of personal data.

sections.heading: Your rights as a data subject
paragraphs: Under the PDPA, you have the following rights in relation to your personal data held by us:
bullets: The right to access and obtain a copy of your personal data
The right to request correction or updating of your personal data
The right to request erasure or suspension of use of your personal data
The right to object to processing and to withdraw your consent
The right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority

sections.heading: Changes to this policy
paragraphs: We may revise this policy in response to changes in law or in our services. We will announce any material changes on this website.

sections.heading: Contact
paragraphs: If you have any questions about this policy or wish to exercise the rights above, please contact us at contact@miraibizlab.co.th.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: How we handle your personal data

hero.title:
In Bangkok,
right by your side.
hero.description: We are the on-the-ground partner for Japanese SMEs taking on Thailand.

message.title: Message from leadership
message.name: Takuro Yoshida
body: MIRAI BizLab exists to be the operational footing that helps Japanese companies launch and grow in Thailand.
Crossing borders means crossing language, regulation, and culture. We unwind those gaps with a fully trilingual team and fifteen years of local experience, so your decisions can move a step faster.

Tidy numbers are not the goal — they are the starting line for smart decisions. We take on accounting, tax, company setup, and consulting as one package so you can keep your focus on the core business.
Share with us what you want to achieve in Thailand. We will walk with you well past the entry point.

rows.label: Head Office
rows.value: 954/1724 The Metropolis Building, 3rd Floor, Room No. M3, Moo. 9, Thepharak, Muang Samutprakarn, Samutprakarn 10270

rows.label: Bangkok Office
rows.value: No. 6/5, H Building, 5th Floor, Soi Promsri, Khlong Tan Nuea, Vadhana, Bangkok 10110

rows.label: Business
rows.value: Accounting & tax, company setup, management consulting

rows.label: Languages
rows.value: Japanese / English / ภาษาไทย

items.label: Mission
items.title: Build the footing for ambition.
items.description: Make it possible for Japanese SMEs to operate in Thailand with both feet firmly on the ground — across accounting, tax, and management.

items.label: Vision
items.title: The right adviser for Bangkok, Thailand.
items.description: When a Japanese company is about to make a move in Bangkok, MIRAI BizLab is the first name they reach out to.

items.label: Values
items.title: Fast · Approachable · Honest.
items.description: Fast in response, easy to reach out to, honest with people — the obvious things, delivered at a refreshing pace.

hero.title:
We take on
the business behind your business.
hero.description: Accounting & tax, company setup, and management consulting — the operational reality of running in Thailand, handled as one.

items.title: Company Setup
items.summary: BOI, local entities, representative offices — from entry scheme design to bank account opening.
items.problem: Too many entry options, with complex assumptions around tax, labor, and shareholding, stall the decision.
items.solution: We design the right entry scheme from your business outcome backwards — and own BOI, registration, bank accounts, and visas end to end.
items.pricing: From THB 75,000 per project (depending on scheme)

items.title: Accounting & Tax
items.summary: Monthly close, tax filings, audits — with Japanese-language support and reporting available. Operations designed to serve both your Japan HQ and local branch.
items.problem: Accounting handled solely by local staff drifts away from HQ-ready reporting, and tax risk becomes hard to see.
items.solution: We design operations that satisfy both TFRS and your HQ's management accounting needs, accelerating monthly close and visibility through cloud accounting.

items.title: Accounting System Implementation
items.summary: Cloud accounting / ERP selection, chart-of-accounts design, and month-end automation that works for both local ops and Japan HQ.
items.problem: You want to roll out a cloud or ERP, but no one can design a setup that satisfies local operations and HQ-grade management accounting at once. Manual work and Excel survive after go-live.
items.solution: We evaluate Thai accounting software such as PEAK / FlowAccount / Express against your operating needs and budget, redesign chart-of-accounts and department codes, and build a flow that automates up to 80% of monthly close.
deliverables: Training for local staff and HQ accounting
items.pricing: From THB 150,000 per project (varies by scope and target system)

items.title: A Sounding Board for Owners
items.summary: Bring us the numbers, the organization, the on-the-ground worries — we frame them with an objective eye and help you put solutions into practice.
items.problem: Tough calls to make, but no one local to talk them through — and no clear person to turn to with worries about the numbers or the organization.
items.solution: We listen to an owner's concerns with an outside perspective, frame the real issues, and work out the next moves together — from management accounting and KPIs to organizational and on-the-ground challenges, all the way to execution.
deliverables: A sounding board to frame your management issues
Management accounting & KPI design (segment / product / store)
Hands-on support putting solutions into practice

cta.title: Not sure which service fits? Talk it through first.
cta.description: We will sort through the combinations and priorities together on a first call.

hero.title:
Stories of ambition,
behind the numbers.
hero.description: We specialize in supporting Japanese SMEs in Thailand, particularly multi-outlet, operations-heavy businesses. From building accounting frameworks to day-to-day bookkeeping and payroll, market entry and exit, here are cases where we worked alongside our clients in line with Thai practice.

hero.title:
Field-tested
operating know-how.
hero.description: Accounting, legal, Thailand entry know-how, and trends — delivered at the resolution we actually work at.

hero.title:

currently hiring.

notice.title: Open positions are temporarily on hold.
notice.description: We are not accepting applications at the moment. If you'd like to be notified when hiring resumes, please reach out via the contact form.

office.title: Our Bangkok office
office.description: Five minutes from Sukhumvit station, our daylit office is where Japanese, Thai, and international colleagues think out loud every day.

positions.title: For reference: roles we typically hire for
positions.subtitle: When hiring resumes, we plan to focus on the following positions.

items.title: Accounting Staff
items.summary: Handle monthly bookkeeping, tax-filing support, and client communication for our Japanese clientele. Japanese required; working English or Thai a plus. 1–3 years of accounting experience.

items.title: Accounting Consultant (Japanese national)
items.summary: Own management accounting design, monthly business reviews, KPI builds, and performance-improvement projects for Japanese SMEs. 3+ years of accounting experience in Japan; business-level English.

items.title: Accounting Consultant (Thai national)
items.summary: Lead accounting operations design for local entities, TFRS-based monthly close, and Thai tax compliance (VAT / withholding / corporate). Native Thai, business English; Japanese a strong plus.

apply.title: Stay in touch
apply.description: If you'd like updates on future openings or want to discuss your career, please reach us through the contact form.

hero.title:
Start with a
short conversation.
